title: LandShift is proud to announce the launch of its Nature-Based Solutions (NbS) Library
content: The NbS Library is an interactive platform built on the systematic identification and curation of implemented NbS cases across Europe. Each case is categorised by ecosystem type, land-use challenge, and solution approach.
snippet: The NbS Library is an interactive platform built on the systematic identification and curation of implemented NbS cases across Europe. Each case is categorised by ecosystem type, land-use challenge, a

title: An ‘ordinary’ storm with extraordinary impacts: what made Wellington’s deluge so intense?
content: The most damaging rainfall often depends on processes playing out over just a few kilometres, or even less, where small variations in temperature, moisture and wind can determine whether one place is inundated while another escapes relatively lightly.

title: Report: Managing natural hazards and climate risks in elections
content: This report surveys the risk that meteorological and geological events pose to elections and offers an analysis of the strategies that electoral management bodies (EMBs) around the world have put in place to safeguard electoral processes.
snippet: This report surveys the risk that meteorological and geological events pose to elections and offers an analysis of the strategies that electoral management bodies (EMBs) around the world have put in p

title: The impact of natural hazards on elections
content: At least 94 election events across 52 countries have been disrupted by natural hazards between 2006 and 2025.
snippet: At least 94 election events across 52 countries have been disrupted by natural hazards between 2006 and 2025.

title: Landshift
content: LandShift is a European research initiative advancing climate-resilient land management by bridging scientific innovation, community engagement, and policy action.
snippet: LandShift is a European research initiative advancing climate-resilient land management by bridging scientific innovation, community engagement, and policy action.

title: Bridging the watershed–urban disconnect: The interplay of risk perception, self-efficacy, and institutional trust in flood preparedness in Pakistan
content: This study examines the disconnect between watershed-scale flood generation and community-scale risk perception and preparedness in Sindh, Pakistan.
snippet: This study examines the disconnect between watershed-scale flood generation and community-scale risk perception and preparedness in Sindh, Pakistan.

title: Climate risk and adaptation assessment for the Laguna Lake Area: an integrated approach to climate-resilient infrastructure planning
content: This report summarizes the results of a climate risk and adaptation assessment of the Laguna Lake area in the Philippines to help inform climate-resilient planning and investment.
snippet: This report summarizes the results of a climate risk and adaptation assessment of the Laguna Lake area in the Philippines to help inform climate-resilient planning and investment.

title: Protection, Gender and Inclusion (PGI) legislative framework related to Disaster Risk Management in the Eastern Caribbean
content: PIRAC – French Red Cross invites you to a webinar dedicated to decoding laws and policies related to PGI in disaster risk management in the Caribbean, led by Marjorie Thimon, PGI Officer.
snippet: PIRAC – French Red Cross invites you to a webinar dedicated to decoding laws and policies related to PGI in disaster risk management in the Caribbean, led by Marjorie Thimon, PGI Officer.

title: Scaling up investment for a resilient power sector: the role of robust disaster-risk financing
content: This Live Wire delves into the insurance prong of the 5 I’s strategy in the context of power system resilience.
snippet: This Live Wire delves into the insurance prong of the 5 I’s strategy in the context of power system resilience.

title: Including persons with disabilities in emergency preparedness and response
content: The report presents a regional diagnostic of disability-inclusive emergency preparedness and response across 38 countries.
snippet: The report presents a regional diagnostic of disability-inclusive emergency preparedness and response across 38 countries.

title: Hurricane Ida’s blackout-heatwave compound risk in a changing climate
content: In this study, the authors employ projections of tropical cyclones (TCs), sea level rise, and heatwaves, in conjunction with power system resilience modeling, to evaluate historical and future TC-blackout-heatwave compound risk in Louisiana, US.
snippet: In this study, the authors employ projections of tropical cyclones (TCs), sea level rise, and heatwaves, in conjunction with power system resilience modeling, to evaluate historical and future TC-blac

title: HERA's heat science hotline
content: HERA has launched the Heat Science Hotline —a free, rapid-response service connecting policymakers, community organizations, journalists, and donors directly to a multidisciplinary board of leading global heat, climate, and health experts.
snippet: HERA has launched the Heat Science Hotline —a free, rapid-response service connecting policymakers, community organizations, journalists, and donors directly to a multidisciplinary board of leading gl

title: Managing agricultural credit risk in the face of natural disasters: Lessons from Sub-Saharan Africa, Asia, and the Americas
content: This study aims to assess how financial institutions (FIs) with a sizable agricultural loan portfolio manage credit risk arising from disasters.
snippet: This study aims to assess how financial institutions (FIs) with a sizable agricultural loan portfolio manage credit risk arising from disasters.

title: An ambitious global goal on adaptation for heritage
content: This paper assesses the Belém Adaptation Indicators adopted at COP30 under the UAE Framework for Global Climate Resilience to clarify strengths and gaps.
snippet: This paper assesses the Belém Adaptation Indicators adopted at COP30 under the UAE Framework for Global Climate Resilience to clarify strengths and gaps.

title: Climate futures require politics
content: The purpose of this commentary is to suggest steps to improve the incorporation of political development in scenarios during the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s (IPCC) seventh assessment cycle (AR7) and beyond.
snippet: The purpose of this commentary is to suggest steps to improve the incorporation of political development in scenarios during the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s (IPCC) seventh assessment c

title: Bhutan learns from New Zealand’s experience in disaster-resilient infrastructure
content: Ten engineers and architects from Bhutan spent six days in Christchurch, enhancing their expertise in damage assessment and retrofitting of critical urban infrastructure, drawing lessons from the city’s post-quake reconstruction lessons.
snippet: Ten engineers and architects from Bhutan spent six days in Christchurch, enhancing their expertise in damage assessment and retrofitting of critical urban infrastructure, drawing lessons from the city

title: Simple ocean-based model forecasts El Niño skillfully, points to a strong event ahead
content: Researchers from the University of Hawai‘i at Mānoa recently published a study showing that they can skillfully predict El Niño and La Niña 15 months ahead of time.
snippet: Researchers from the University of Hawai‘i at Mānoa recently published a study showing that they can skillfully predict El Niño and La Niña 15 months ahead of time.

title: Farming podcast: building resilience through soil and water management
content: In this episode, Catchment Sensitive Farming (CSF) adviser Hannah Barrett speaks to two arable farmers. Charlie Ennals farms in Norfolk and Thomas Gent farms in Cambridgeshire.
snippet: In this episode, Catchment Sensitive Farming (CSF) adviser Hannah Barrett speaks to two arable farmers. Charlie Ennals farms in Norfolk and Thomas Gent farms in Cambridgeshire.

title: Weather radar data reveal the dynamics of rapidly spreading wildfires
content: Research demonstrates the use of operational weather radar measurements to track long-range ember fallout and rapid spread of intense wildfires.
snippet: Research demonstrates the use of operational weather radar measurements to track long-range ember fallout and rapid spread of intense wildfires.

title: Learning matters: Navigating the first funding cycle for responding to loss and damage
content: Loss and Damage Fund launches first cycle: how countries access funding, build climate data systems, and act under Barbados Implementation Modalities.
snippet: Loss and Damage Fund launches first cycle: how countries access funding, build climate data systems, and act under Barbados Implementation Modalities.

title: New website helps landowners track wildfire recovery, view history
content: The resource — the first of its kind focused on working grasslands — is designed to track and monitor long-term recovery while supporting farmers, ranchers and land managers as they restore Nebraska’s native grasslands.
snippet: The resource — the first of its kind focused on working grasslands — is designed to track and monitor long-term recovery while supporting farmers, ranchers and land managers as they restore Nebraska’s

title: Extreme heat and agriculture
content: The frequency, intensity and duration of extreme heat events have risen sharply over the past half century, with worrying impacts on agrifood systems and landscapes, according to this report.
snippet: The frequency, intensity and duration of extreme heat events have risen sharply over the past half century, with worrying impacts on agrifood systems and landscapes, according to this report.

title: New online hub launched to support rural flood resilience
content: The platform brings together updated guidance for rural communities, farmers and landowners, covering both flood preparedness and recovery. Additional practical guidance, including advice on improving the resilience of farm buildings to be added.
snippet: The platform brings together updated guidance for rural communities, farmers and landowners, covering both flood preparedness and recovery. Additional practical guidance, including advice on improving

title: Extreme heat pushes agrifood systems to the brink
content: Extreme heat events threaten the livelihoods, health and labour productivity of over a billion people. Agricultural workers and agrifood systems are on the frontlines.
snippet: Extreme heat events threaten the livelihoods, health and labour productivity of over a billion people. Agricultural workers and agrifood systems are on the frontlines.

title: Rural Services Network
content: The Rural Services Network (RSN) is the National Champion for Rural Services across England, working to ensure that the voices, needs and aspirations of rural, coastal and small-town communities are heard at the highest levels of government.

title: Weather and climate-induced multi-hazard futures: Forecast, communication, and preparedness for society
content: This collection focuses on the social dimensions of these evolving multi-hazard risks in a changing climate—how weather and climate information is produced, translated, communicated, and acted upon within diverse decision contexts.

title: RFQ- Provision of PRINCE2® foundation training and managing successful programmes (MSP®) introduction
content: CDRI seeks quotations from qualified and accredited training providers to deliver PRINCE2® Foundation training, an introduction to Managing Successful Programmes (MSP®), and a tailored contextualisation session for CDRI Secretariat delivery personnel.

title: Wildfires: origins, meteorological drivers and mitigation strategies
content: This conference is part of the III Conference Cycle of the Postgraduate Program in “Investigation and Security in Catastrophes, Crises and Accidents” and addresses the growing risks associated with wildfires in the context of climate change.

title: After the floods, the GBV: The double tragedy for women in Kenya’s informal settlements
content: When the rains finally came to Nairobi at the beginning of March, many residents were grateful. But then the nightly showers turned torrential, and residents across the city’s informal settlements began to worry.

title: Fires and falsehoods in California
content: Security actors around the world are beginning to recognize the risks posed by information manipulation surrounding extreme weather, climate resilience, and the clean energy transition
snippet: Security actors around the world are beginning to recognize the risks posed by information manipulation surrounding extreme weather, climate resilience, and the clean energy transition

title: Extreme climate events increase heart disease risk, study reveals
content: Analysis in the American Journal of Preventive Medicine shows how heatwaves, extreme cold, and heavy rain impact the incidence of heart disease in older populations.
snippet: Analysis in the American Journal of Preventive Medicine shows how heatwaves, extreme cold, and heavy rain impact the incidence of heart disease in older populations.

title: UK advances surface water flood forecasting
content: The UK has taken a significant step forward in improving its ability to forecast surface water flooding, says the government, as a three-year programme to enhance national capability reaches completion.
snippet: The UK has taken a significant step forward in improving its ability to forecast surface water flooding, says the government, as a three-year programme to enhance national capability reaches completio

title: Increasing heat can boost malnutrition among children
content: A study in Brazil links higher temperatures to worse nutrition measures in vulnerable children.
snippet: A study in Brazil links higher temperatures to worse nutrition measures in vulnerable children.

title: To strengthen climate resilience, focus on social protection
content: How do we prevent the climate crisis from reversing decades of progress on poverty reduction?
snippet: How do we prevent the climate crisis from reversing decades of progress on poverty reduction?

title: The fire forecast: AI model accurately predicts the spread of wildfires in real time
content: By combining high-resolution satellite data, terrain data and realistic fire simulations, USC Viterbi researchers have developed a reconstruction and prediction tool for making informed decisions when tackling catastrophic wildfires.
snippet: By combining high-resolution satellite data, terrain data and realistic fire simulations, USC Viterbi researchers have developed a reconstruction and prediction tool for making informed decisions when

title: Why climate models and ocean observations diverge, and what it means for rain and drought
content: Scientific models have predicted that climate change will drive oceans in the Northern Hemisphere to warm faster than oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. However, observational data over the last 70 years show the opposite.
snippet: Scientific models have predicted that climate change will drive oceans in the Northern Hemisphere to warm faster than oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. However, observational data over the last 70 ye

title: AI framework pinpoints global drivers of extreme heatwaves
content: As the climate warms, extreme events such as heatwaves, heavy rainfall and droughts are becoming more frequent, more extensive and more damaging. For scientists, the key question is no longer only whether these events will intensify in a warmer world.

title: Cold weather linked to 40,000 extra heart deaths each year in the U.S.
content: A large U.S. study shows cold weather is linked to far more cardiovascular deaths than heat, accounting for tens of thousands of extra deaths each year.
snippet: A large U.S. study shows cold weather is linked to far more cardiovascular deaths than heat, accounting for tens of thousands of extra deaths each year.

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model (Sri Lanka)
content: The purpose of this consultancy is to facilitate the development and documentation of a country (i.e., SLPP)-specific Localization Model, capturing how localization has been defined, operationalized, and institutionalized through the partnership.

title: Tsunami advisory lifted after Japan earthquake but warnings for potential stronger quake issued
content: The Japanese government has warned of a slightly heightened risk of a megaquake in the next week after a strong 7.4-magnitude earthquake struck off the country’s northeastern coast on Monday
snippet: The Japanese government has warned of a slightly heightened risk of a megaquake in the next week after a strong 7.4-magnitude earthquake struck off the country’s northeastern coast on Monday
